en.text: , for them that defile themselves with mankind
; given: or, ready to quarrel, and offer wrong, as one in wine
. a novice: or, one newly come to the faith
let them use the office of a deacon
they that have used the office of a deacon
of that which is to come
. little: or, for a little time
. to all: or, in all things
for those of his own house
they have begun to wax wanton against
. to speak: Gr. for their railing
, proud: or, a fool doting: or, sick
. Perverse: or, Gallings one of another
that which is committed to thy trust
